# Technical Specification: Accessibility, Animation, and Cross-Device Quality — Modern Landing Page Redesign

## 1. Overview

This is the integration quality story for the complete landing page redesign. It governs three cross-cutting concerns that span every section built in the preceding stories:

1. **Orchestrated stagger reveal animation** — a single, shared system initiated once per page load
2. **`prefers-reduced-motion` compliance** — handled globally through CSS token zeroing, not per section
3. **WCAG AA accessibility pass** — contrast ratios, keyboard navigation, screen reader compatibility across all sections

**Scope and constraints:**
- No new Blazor components or data files; this story validates the integrated system
- All implementation targets were established in individual section stories; this story identifies any gaps via a full-page audit
- Where gaps are found, fixes go back to the owning section's files (not a new file)

---

## 2. Animation System

### 2.1 Architecture (established in Hero Section Spec)

The reveal system consists of:

| Piece | Location | Role |
|-------|----------|------|
| `.reveal-item` CSS class | `wwwroot/app.css` | Starts elements at `opacity: 0; transform: translateY(1.25rem)` |
| `.is-visible` CSS class | `wwwroot/app.css` | Transitions element to `opacity: 1; transform: none` |
| `--reveal-delay` CSS custom property | Per-element inline style | Controls stagger offset per element |
| `IntersectionObserver` script | `wwwroot/app.js` | Adds `.is-visible` when element enters viewport |
| `prefers-reduced-motion` token zeroing | `wwwroot/app.css` `:root` | Sets all timing tokens to `0ms`; transitions become instant |

### 2.2 Blazor Re-render Integration

After `OnInitializedAsync` completes and `StateHasChanged` re-renders, newly injected DOM elements are not observed by the `IntersectionObserver` created at `DOMContentLoaded`. The component must re-invoke the observer after first render:

```razor
@inject IJSRuntime JS

protected override async Task OnAfterRenderAsync(bool firstRender)
{
    if (firstRender)
    {
        await JS.InvokeVoidAsync("reObserveRevealItems");
    }
}
```

Corresponding JavaScript function in `app.js`:

```javascript
window.reObserveRevealItems = function () {
    document.querySelectorAll('.reveal-item:not(.is-visible)').forEach((el) => {
        revealObserver.observe(el);
    });
};
```

> The `revealObserver` must be promoted to a module-level variable (not local to the IIFE) to be accessible from `reObserveRevealItems`.

### 2.3 Stagger Delay Reference Table

Consistent delay values used across all sections:

| Position in section | `--reveal-delay` |
|---------------------|-----------------|
| Section heading     | `0ms`           |
| First element       | `80ms`          |
| Second element      | `160ms`         |
| Third element       | `240ms`         |
| Fourth element      | `320ms`         |

Section headings always start at `0ms`. Sub-elements increment by `80ms` (the `--stagger-step` token value).

### 2.4 `prefers-reduced-motion` Strategy

- **Zero the tokens, not the elements.** Setting `--motion-fast`, `--motion-normal`, `--motion-slow`, and `--stagger-step` to `0ms` in the `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` `:root` block makes every `transition` and `animation` using those tokens instantaneous — no `transition: none` override needed per element.
- **Ensure elements start visible.** The `.reveal-item` CSS must also set `opacity: 1; transform: none; transition: none` when the media query matches:

```css
@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) {
    .reveal-item {
        opacity: 1;
        transform: none;
        transition: none;
    }
}
```

This ensures that even if `IntersectionObserver` fires late (e.g. in slow network conditions), elements are never invisible.

---

## 3. Accessibility Requirements

### 3.1 Heading Hierarchy

The full page heading hierarchy must be:

```
h1 — Hero headline (single instance, "Bespoke Software Solutions" or site name)
  h2 — "Our Services"
    h3 — Each service card title (×6)
  h2 — "How We Work"
    h3 — Each step title (×4) [implicit via ol semantics, but explicit h3 preferred]
  h2 — "Technologies"
    h3 — Each capability band label
  h2 — "Ready to work together?" (Contact CTA)
```

> **Validation:** Run `document.querySelectorAll('h1,h2,h3,h4,h5,h6')` in browser DevTools to confirm no heading levels are skipped and the `h1` appears exactly once.

### 3.2 Landmark Regions

Every major page section must have a corresponding ARIA landmark to allow screen reader users to skip between regions:

| Section | Element | Required attribute |
|---------|---------|--------------------|
| Hero | `<section>` | `aria-labelledby="hero-headline"` |
| Services | `<section>` | `aria-labelledby="services-heading"` |
| How We Work | `<section>` | `aria-labelledby="process-heading"` |
| Technologies | `<section>` | `aria-labelledby="technologies-heading"` |
| Contact CTA | `<section>` | `aria-labelledby="contact-heading"` |
| Footer | `<footer>` | `role="contentinfo"` |

### 3.3 Decorative Elements

All decorative elements must use `aria-hidden="true"`:

| Element | Location |
|---------|----------|
| `.hero-accent-rule` | Hero section |
| `.step-number` text inside `<div>` | How We Work |
| Emoji icon `.service-icon` within `<span>` | Services section |
| CSS `::before`/`::after` pseudo-elements | All sections (automatically inaccessible) |

### 3.4 Keyboard Navigation

All interactive elements must be keyboard accessible:

| Element | Expected keyboard behaviour |
|---------|-----------------------------|
| `.btn-primary`, `.btn-secondary` (hero CTAs) | Tab to focus, Enter to activate, visible `focus-visible` ring |
| `.btn-cta` (contact email link) | Tab to focus, Enter to activate |
| Footer nav links | Tab to focus, Enter to activate |
| Footer social links | Tab to focus, Enter to activate, new tab opens |
| Service card grid | Cards not interactive (no `tabindex`); no focus trap |
| Technology badges | Badges not interactive; no `tabindex` |

> **Tab order:** Must follow the DOM reading order (hero → services → how we work → technologies → contact → footer). Do not use positive `tabindex` values.

### 3.5 WCAG AA Contrast Ratios

Minimum ratio requirements and the element/background combinations to verify:

| Text element | Background | Required ratio | Notes |
|--------------|------------|----------------|-------|
| Hero headline | `--color-surface-base` (#f4f4f4) | 4.5:1 | Large text ≥24px: 3:1 acceptable |
| Hero sub-copy | `--color-surface-base` | 4.5:1 | |
| Value chip text | `--color-surface-card` (#fff) | 4.5:1 | |
| `.btn-primary` label | Accent gradient | 4.5:1 | Verify on darkest point of gradient |
| `.btn-secondary` label | `--color-surface-base` | 4.5:1 | |
| Service card name + description | `--color-surface-card` (#fff) | 4.5:1 | |
| Step title + description | `--color-surface-base` | 4.5:1 | |
| Step number (`01`–`04`) | Accent gradient | 3:1 | Large bold text |
| Technology badge text | `--color-surface-base` | 4.5:1 | |
| Contact CTA heading + sub | Accent gradient | 4.5:1 | Verify on darkest point of gradient |
| `.btn-cta` label | `--color-surface-card` (#fff) | 4.5:1 | |
| Footer nav/social links | `--color-surface-base` | 4.5:1 | Also verify hover state |
| Footer copyright | `--color-surface-base` | 4.5:1 | |
| `--color-text-muted` (#767676) on white | White | 4.54:1 ✓ | Just passes; do not darken the background |

> **Tooling:** Use the browser's Accessibility panel or [WebAIM Contrast Checker](https://webaim.org/resources/contrastchecker/). For gradient backgrounds, check contrast at the darkest point of the gradient.

---

## 4. Responsive Quality

### 4.1 Breakpoints

| Name | Range | Grid behaviour |
|------|-------|----------------|
| Mobile | < 768px | Single column for all sections |
| Tablet | 768px – 1023px | Hero: single col; Services: 2-col; Process: 2×2; Technologies: 1-col |
| Desktop | ≥ 1024px | Hero: 2-col; Services: 3-col; Process: 4-col horizontal; Technologies: 2-col |

### 4.2 Viewport Test Matrix

| Viewport | Test |
|----------|------|
| 320px | No horizontal overflow; all text readable; no truncation |
| 375px | No horizontal overflow; CTAs full-width on mobile |
| 768px | Hero adapts; services 2-col; no overflow |
| 1024px | Desktop layouts engage; process connector visible |
| 1440px | Content centred; max-width containers respect `max-width: 1200px` |

### 4.3 Touch Target Sizes

All interactive elements on mobile viewports must meet the WCAG 2.1 SC 2.5.5 recommended minimum of 44×44px:

| Element | Mobile size | Enforcement method |
|---------|-------------|-------------------|
| `.btn-primary` / `.btn-secondary` | Min 44px height via `padding` | CSS `min-height: 44px` on `.btn` |
| `.btn-cta` | Full width; min 44px height | `width: 100%; min-height: 44px` |
| Footer nav/social links | 44px touch target | `padding: 0.625rem` (≈ 10px + line-height ≈ 44px) |

---

## 5. Testing Requirements

### Playwright — Animation Tests

| Test | Setup | Action | Expected |
|------|-------|--------|----------|
| Stagger reveals on load | Default settings | Navigate to `/` | `.hero-left .reveal-item` has class `is-visible` after load |
| Below-fold section reveals on scroll | Navigate, then scroll | Scroll to `#services` | Service cards gain `is-visible` when entering viewport |
| No flicker before reveal | Record timeline | Page load | No frame where content is partially visible then hidden |
| Reduced motion — all visible | `emulateMedia({reducedMotion:'reduce'})` | Navigate to `/` | All `.reveal-item` elements have `opacity: 1` immediately |
| Reduced motion — hover transitions disabled | `emulateMedia({reducedMotion:'reduce'})` | Hover a service card | Card does not move; no transition applied |

### Playwright — Accessibility Tests (via `@axe-core/playwright`)

| Test | Action | Expected |
|------|--------|----------|
| Zero critical violations | Run `checkA11y()` on full page | No violations at `critical` impact level |
| No WCAG AA contrast failures | Run `checkA11y()` with WCAG AA rules | No contrast violations |
| Skip navigation / landmarks | Tab from address bar | First Tab stops at a meaningful interactive element; all sections are reachable as landmarks |
| Single `h1` | Run `checkA11y()` | No "page-has-heading-one" violation |
| Heading hierarchy | Inspect heading outline | No skipped heading levels |

### Manual Accessibility Checks

| Check | Method | Expected |
|-------|--------|----------|
| Screen reader section navigation | NVDA/VoiceOver, use virtual cursor | Each section named correctly by heading when navigating by landmark |
| Screen reader hero value chips | Navigate to hero chips | Chips read as plain text, not as interactive or structural elements |
| Screen reader service card list | Navigate to services | "List of 6 items" announced; each card announced with name and description |
| Screen reader process steps | Navigate to How We Work | "Ordered list of 4 items" announced; steps described in sequence |

---

## 6. Implementation Notes

### Key Integration Risks

1. **`IntersectionObserver` fires before Blazor renders:** If `app.js` runs before `OnInitializedAsync` populates the component, `.reveal-item` elements may not exist yet. Mitigated by `OnAfterRenderAsync` calling `reObserveRevealItems`. Ensure the JS function is idempotent (safe to call multiple times).

2. **CSS specificity conflicts between `app.css` and `LandingPage.razor.css`:** Blazor scoped CSS adds a `b-{hash}` attribute selector to all rules in `.razor.css` files. Scoped rules will be more specific than global rules in `app.css`. If a scoped rule ever overrides `--motion-*` tokens or `.reveal-item`, the reduced-motion global fallback may be bypassed. Verify no scoped rule re-declares transition properties without a `prefers-reduced-motion` guard.

3. **Gradient background contrast fluctuates:** Linear gradients have variable lightness across their range. WCAG contrast checks should target the darkest point of the accent gradient, not the midpoint or a sampled average.

### Build Order for This Story

This story has no implementation work of its own — it is a QA pass. It should be executed after all 5 section stories are complete:

1. Verify `revealObserver` is module-level in `app.js` (not scoped inside an IIFE)
2. Verify `reObserveRevealItems` is exposed on `window`
3. Verify `OnAfterRenderAsync(firstRender: true)` calls `reObserveRevealItems`
4. Run the full Playwright test suite
5. Run `@axe-core/playwright` accessibility checks
6. Manually verify screen reader experience with NVDA or VoiceOver
7. Manually verify responsive layout at all breakpoint widths

### Tooling Recommendations

| Tool | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `@axe-core/playwright` | Automated WCAG AA checks in Playwright tests |
| Chrome DevTools Accessibility panel | Visual heading outline, contrast inspection |
| WebAIM Contrast Checker | Manual gradient background contrast verification |
| Chrome/Edge `prefers-reduced-motion` emulation | `DevTools → Rendering → Emulate CSS media feature` |
| NVDA (Windows) / VoiceOver (macOS/iOS) | Manual screen reader verification |

---
